## Meta Announces First Major Canadian Data Center, Extending AI Infrastructure Buildout Internationally

META's Canadian buildout signals continued hyperscaler commitment to AI infrastructure expansion and geographic diversification — broadly bullish for the data center supply chain (power, cooling, networking). The sovereign AI angle may foreshadow regulatory tailwinds or localization requirements that shape where future capex lands.

- Meta is constructing its first large data center in Canada, marking a geographic expansion of its AI compute footprint.
- The move reflects the hyperscaler pattern of internationalizing AI infrastructure beyond US borders.
- A cross-border sovereign AI deployment dimension is present, with potential Canadian regulatory and policy implications.
- No capex figure, capacity specs, or timeline are named in the headline.
